## Paper

Bid rotation and collusion in repeated auctions

## World

auction_house

## Institution

ISER, Osaka University

## Agent type

bidders

## Theoretical benchmark

equilibrium

## Learning setup

dynamic

## Metrics

payoff

## Main result

collusion scheme is an equilibrium if truth-telling is incentive compatible and obedience to the instructions is rational for each bidder

## What they prove

existence of a collusion scheme that coordinates bids in a dynamic environment

## What they only simulate

no side payment of money

## What they do NOT test

static collusion schemes with only current reported signals

## What we need to reproduce

the classical benchmark, prior learning setup, metrics, and what this project must reproduce

## How our project differs

dynamic scheme yields a strictly higher payoff to the bidders than any static collusion scheme
